TSLA: China Is Investigating EV Makers As Owners Say Their Cars Lost Up To 125 Miles Of Range After Updates—And. Chinese authorities are investigating EV manufacturers after owners reported significant range reduction (up to 125 miles) and longer charging times following software updates, suspected to be "battery locking." While Tesla and BYD were among the companies reportedly summoned, they deny involvement. This probe comes amid varying sales performances for these companies in the Chinese market.

Elon Musk's Vocal Social Media Critic Bought A Cybertruck For Its 'Safety,' Here's What The Tesla CEO Had To Say About It. Tesla CEO Elon Musk has defended the Cybertruck's design and safety features after critic Brian Krassenstein purchased one, citing family safety and fuel economy. Musk highlighted the vehicle's "bulletproof tank" performance and its ultra-hard stainless steel body panels as key advantages. The Cybertruck has received strong safety ratings from the IIHS and NHTSA, and Tesla continues to show high brand loyalty among repeat buyers.

Tesla Once Again Recalls Its RWD Cybertrucks—All 173 of ‘Em. Tesla has issued another recall for all 173 of its rear-wheel-drive Cybertrucks due to a defect in the brake rotor stud holes that could cause wheels to separate. This is the latest of 11 recalls for the discontinued model, though Tesla reports no related collisions, fatalities, or injuries. The company offers a free fix for the issue, which affects models with 18-inch steel wheels.

Tesla recalls every Cybertruck RWD ever sold — all 173 of them — because the wheels could fall off. Tesla has issued a recall for all 173 Cybertruck RWD models ever sold due to a defect where the wheels could fall off. This issue stems from cracks in the brake rotor holes caused by road irregularities, potentially leading to wheel stud separation. The recall comes as Cybertruck sales have reportedly declined significantly, and is concurrent with another recall for over 200,000 other Tesla models due to delayed rearview camera images.

Tesla (TSLA): Top 10 Battery Technology Stock To Buy. Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ: TSLA) is highlighted as one of the top battery technology stocks to buy. The article discusses a recent win for Tesla as the U.S. National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) closed a probe into Model Y steering wheel issues without requiring a recall, though further action may be taken if new defects surface. Needham also reiterated a hold rating on TSLA, citing AI progress and vertical integration but warning about potential challenges to margin outperformance and high capital expenditure.

Weekend Round-Up: Uber's Massive Quarter, Tesla's Recall, Lucid's Earnings, Spirit Airlines' Buyout Plan. This week's business wrap-up highlights significant actions from major companies. Uber reported strong Q1 revenue and adjusted earnings despite missing overall revenue estimates, while Tesla initiated a massive recall of over 218,000 vehicles for a rearview camera issue. Lucid Group's stock dropped following a Q1 revenue miss, and a fan-led buyout plan for Spirit Airlines, inspired by the Green Bay Packers, gained substantial pledges. Toyota, meanwhile, forecasted a significant 22% profit drop due to geopolitical conflicts and U.S. tariffs.

Intel CEO who won over Trump and Musk now needs a breakthrough. Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan has revitalized the struggling chipmaker by forging strategic alliances with tech giants like Apple and Tesla, and even securing a deal with former US President Donald Trump. While these external partnerships have driven Intel's stock to a record high, Tan now faces the challenge of implementing internal changes and product innovations to regain market share in semiconductor manufacturing and leverage the AI boom. He aims to build a strong internal leadership team by the end of June to achieve these goals.

[424B2] BARCLAYS BANK PLC Prospectus Supplement. Barclays Bank PLC is offering contingent coupon, autocallable Notes linked to the common stock of NVIDIA, Palantir Technologies, and Tesla. These Notes offer a significant quarterly contingent coupon of $48.50 per $1,000 (19.40% per annum) if underlying stocks meet specified conditions, but investors risk potential loss of principal if the least performing underlier falls below its barrier value. The offering is subject to Barclays Bank PLC’s creditworthiness and U.K. Bail-in Power regulations, with a neutral impact and sentiment according to Rhea-AI.

This announcement provides investors with details regarding the payout from the ETF's income strategy, which is focused on options related to Tesla stock.

YieldMax Short TSLA Option Income Strategy ETF announces weekly distribution of $0.2369. This distribution is payable on or about May 10, 2024, to shareholders of record as of May 09, 2024. Investors should note the ex-dividend date is May 09, 2024.

Tesla recalls Cybertrucks over fears wheels could fall off while driving. Tesla is recalling 173 Cybertrucks due to a potential defect where wheel studs could separate, leading to wheels detaching. The issue is linked to stress on the brake rotors in vehicles with 18-inch steel wheels, stemming from a pre-production testing oversight. Although one customer reported vibrations and cracked rotors, no crashes or injuries have been reported.

Tesla recalls more than 200,000 EVs, 173 Cybertrucks over safety issues. Tesla is recalling over 200,000 electric vehicles due to a rearview camera software issue that causes the image to appear blank for up to 11 seconds. Additionally, 173 Cybertrucks are being recalled over concerns that cracking in the wheel rotor could lead to the wheel stud separating from the hub, increasing crash risks. This marks another recall for Cybertrucks, with nearly 116,000 recalled in 2025 alone, though Tesla is not aware of any accidents or injuries related to these specific problems.

INTC Stock Hits New All-Time High After Intel, Apple Reportedly Reach Preliminary Chipmaking Agreement. Intel (INTC) shares surged to a new all-time high after reports indicated a preliminary agreement with Apple (AAPL) for Intel to manufacture chips for Apple devices. This deal is expected to significantly boost Intel's Foundry unit, which has been underperforming with a recent $2.4 billion operating loss, and follows Apple CEO Tim Cook's concerns about advanced chip shortages impacting iPhone supply. The news also comes as Intel expands its collaborations with other major tech companies like Tesla and Google for chip manufacturing and AI infrastructure.

Tesla Recalls Cybertrucks Over Concerns Their Wheels Could Come Off. Tesla is recalling 173 Cybertrucks (model years 2024-2026) equipped with 18-inch steel wheels due to a potential issue where wheel studs could separate from the brake rotor, leading to loss of vehicle control. The problem, which Tesla initially identified during pre-production testing but failed to address in early production due to a "change management error," involves cracks forming in the stud holes under certain driving conditions. The recall also sheds light on the very low demand for the discontinued budget Cybertruck variant.
TGT: Mama’s Creations Invited to Attend Upcoming Investor Conferences. Mama's Creations, Inc. announced that its management will attend four investor conferences in Spring 2026, including the BMO Farm to Market Conference and the William Blair Annual Growth Conference. Chairman and CEO Adam L. Michaels will host one-on-one meetings to update investors on the company's progress, including the integration of Crown 1 and new distribution placements at Walmart and Target. The company aims to become a $1 billion deli prepared foods provider.

Target Corp. stock (US87612E1064): Tariff risks and turnaround hopes weigh on shares near 52-week hi. Target Corp. stock, trading near its 52-week high after a 40% climb over the past year, faces pressure from new tariff risks and questions regarding its turnaround strategy. Investors are keenly awaiting the Q1 2027 earnings call on May 20, 2026, for indications that new CEO Michael Fiddelke’s plan is stabilizing margins and boosting comparable-store sales. Despite strong recent performance, longer-term shareholder returns are mixed, and the stock's valuation needs to account for potential downside risks from tariffs and execution challenges.

Mizuho Raises its Price Target on Duke Energy (DUK) to $139. Mizuho increased its price target for Duke Energy (DUK) to $139 from $130, maintaining an Outperform rating after the company reported better-than-expected Q1 adjusted EPS and revenue. Duke Energy reaffirmed its FY26 adjusted EPS outlook, and its Robinson Nuclear Plant recently had its operating license renewed through 2050. Morgan Stanley also adjusted its price target for DUK to $141 from $142, while keeping an Overweight rating.

Target names new design, apparel leaders as CEO pushes style-first turnaround. Target has appointed new leaders in design and apparel as CEO Brian Cornell continues to implement a style-first turnaround strategy. Gena Fox, a 25-year Target veteran, will serve as Senior Vice President of Design, focusing on cultural and trend leadership. Tara Russell has been named Senior Vice President of Apparel and Accessories, having previously contributed to the launch of the design-focused Target SoHo store.

Target sued over coffee creamer that allegedly contains fewer servings than advertised. Target Corporation is facing a class action lawsuit alleging that its Market Pantry Original Coffee Creamer powder misrepresents the number of servings on its label. Plaintiff Astorria Sassano claims that independent testing revealed the product contains at least 13.31% fewer one-teaspoon servings than advertised and that the stated gram equivalency for a serving is inaccurate. The lawsuit, filed in New York federal court, accuses Target of violating consumer protection laws and breaching express warranties.

Target Corp. Stock (US87612E1064): RBC Raises Price Target to $132 Ahead of Q1 Earnings. RBC Capital has raised its price target for Target Corp. to $132 from $130, maintaining an Outperform rating, ahead of its Q1 earnings release. This adjustment reflects increasing confidence in Target's strategic initiatives, including pricing, assortment, and digital engagement, which are expected to improve financial performance. Target shares are currently trading near their 52-week high, indicating positive market sentiment, but investors should be aware of competitive and economic risks in the retail sector.

Snacks sold at Target voluntarily recalled over possible salmonella concerns. Several snack products, including Target's Good & Gather trail mix, have been voluntarily recalled due to potential salmonella contamination in a seasoning mix. The seasoning mix used a dry milk powder that was previously recalled, though the seasoning itself tested negative for salmonella. The manufacturer, John B. Sanfilippo & Son, Inc., has not received any reports of illnesses related to the recalled items.

Snacks sold at Target voluntarily recalled over possible salmonella concerns. Several snack mixes, including Target's Good & Gather brand trail mix and other brands distributed by John B. Sanfilippo & Son, Inc., have been voluntarily recalled due to potential salmonella contamination. The recall stems from a seasoning mix made with dry milk powder from California Dairies, Inc., which has caused previous recalls. While no illnesses have been reported, consumers are advised to check the FDA website for product details and be aware of salmonella symptoms.

The company also announced a quarterly dividend of $0.62 per share, and analysts currently rate the stock as a "Moderate Buy" with a consensus price target of $107.

Starbucks Corp. stock (US8552441094): Q2 2026 beats expectations as shares rise on earnings surprise. Starbucks Corp.'s stock rose after the company reported better-than-expected Q2 2026 results, with quarterly revenue of $9.5 billion and earnings per share of $0.50, surpassing Wall Street forecasts. This positive reaction comes amidst analyst concerns regarding slower top-line growth and margin pressures, indicating that the company's operational turnaround efforts are showing tangible financial improvement. For investors, Starbucks offers exposure to a globally recognized brand but is sensitive to consumer spending and competitive dynamics.

Humana poaches Starbucks data chief for C-suite role. Humana has hired Bhagyesh Phanse, Starbucks' former data chief, for a C-suite role. This strategic move indicates Humana's increased focus on artificial intelligence and data analytics to further its business goals. The appointment highlights the growing importance of data leadership in the healthcare industry.

Chipotle opens Park 11 location in Belleville with Tulfra Real Estate. Chipotle has opened its 110th New Jersey location at Park 11 in Belleville, a project developed by Tulfra Real Estate and The Hampshire Companies. The grand opening was celebrated with local officials and also served as an opportunity for Tulfra to donate to the Legacy Boxing Club, which is rebuilding after a fire. This addition further enhances Park 11 as a vibrant destination, joining other national brands like Starbucks and Wawa.

Five Below Bets On Viral Brands Technology And Ex Party City Stores. Five Below is focusing on viral, influencer-driven product lines like the Love Island Golden Spray Tan Collection and Disney-themed vacation essentials to attract younger shoppers. The company is also expanding by renovating former Party City locations and leveraging advanced analytics and AI for supply chain management. This strategy positions Five Below at the intersection of discount retail and pop culture, aiming for continued relevance through fast-moving trends and accessible pricing.

Yacktman Asset Management LP Decreases Stake in The Walt Disney Company $DIS. The Walt Disney Company stock (US9314271084): Q2 FY26 earnings show revenue growth but lower EPS on prior tax gain comparison. The Walt Disney Company (NYSE: DIS) reported increased revenue but lower earnings per share for its Q2 FY26, largely due to a prior-year one-time tax benefit. Revenue grew 7% to $25.2 billion, driven by subscriptions, affiliate fees, and strong performance in its Experiences segment. The company also returned capital to shareholders through share repurchases and dividends.

The Walt Disney Company to Participate in the MoffettNathanson Media, Internet & Communications Conference. The Walt Disney Company announced that Hugh Johnston, Senior Executive Vice President and CFO, will participate in a question-and-answer session at the MoffettNathanson Media, Internet & Communications Conference on May 14, 2026. The session is scheduled for 1:30 p.m. ET / 10:30 a.m. PT and will be available to stream live on the company's investor relations website. A recording will also be archived on the site.

Analysts’ Top Communication Services Picks: Walt Disney (DIS), TKO Group Holdings (TKO). This article highlights two communication services stocks, Walt Disney (DIS) and TKO Group Holdings (TKO), that recently received bullish sentiments from analysts. Rosenblatt Securities maintained a Buy rating for Disney with a Strong Buy consensus, while Roth MKM maintained a Buy rating for TKO Group Holdings, also with a Strong Buy consensus. Both companies show significant upside potential according to the analysts.

Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce Launches Disney+ Streaming Partnership. Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce (CIBC) has announced a new partnership with Disney+ to offer streaming benefits to eligible CIBC credit card holders. This collaboration aims to provide added value and entertainment options to CIBC clients. Further details about eligibility and benefit redemption are expected to be released by CIBC.

Disney to livestream CFO Hugh Johnston at May 14 conference. The Walt Disney Company announced that its Senior Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, Hugh Johnston, will participate in a question-and-answer session at the MoffettNathanson Media, Internet & Communications Conference on May 14, 2026. The session is scheduled for approximately 1:30 p.m. ET / 10:30 a.m. PT and will be livestreamed on Disney's investor relations website. A recording of the event will also be archived online.

Landmark Oasis Documentary Film by Steven Knight Coming to Theatres and Disney+ this Year. A new documentary about the legendary British band Oasis, created by Steven Knight and directed by Dylan Southern and Will Lovelace, is set to release this year. The film will cover Liam and Noel Gallagher's 2025 reunion tour, "Oasis Live ’25," offering an uplifting account with behind-the-scenes access and the first joint interviews with the brothers in over two decades. It will debut in select IMAX and cinemas globally on September 11, followed by streaming on Disney+ internationally, and Hulu and Disney+ in the U.S.
NKE: NIKE, Inc. stock (US6541061031): Dividend affirmed as analysts downgrade outlook. NIKE, Inc. has affirmed its quarterly dividend at $0.41 per share despite recent analyst downgrades and ongoing revenue pressure, particularly in North America. The company faces challenges including weaker athletic footwear demand, softened direct and digital sales, and a shift towards lower-margin wholesale channels, leading to a 19% stock decline over the past year. Investors are advised to monitor quarterly results, margin trends, and regional demand as key indicators.
